transcript: What a race in prospect here. The blue ribboned event, the men's 1500m. Josh Kerr, one of three Britons in this final. But there's the favourite. His Pashiro Djibouti came through really late. And this guy has made a career out of coming through late. Lewandowski, a possible dark horse. He's the European indoor champion. If it's slow and gets tactical, watch him burn on the outside. Craig Engels, sporting the mullet. He looks like a 1980s high school idol he's lightning-quick on the last lap 51.9 to beat Sentrovic for the US title he's got the haircut and the speed to match Berglund has lowered some massive national records for Sweden this year Jake Whiteman Commonwealth and European bronze last year sixth in the world indoors he scraped into the final by the skin of his teeth a slight tactical misjudgment in the semis today right now there will be absolutely no room for error from anyone the final of the men's 1500 meters a huge roar from the crowd as this epic final gets underway it's absolutely loaded here coming up onto the shoulder of the forward leaning timothy chariot you can always tell it's chariot because that head leans forward but so effectively this year other than losing here to elijah menango his great friend and who's injured, he hasn't put a foot wrong in 2019. And Jenny Meadows, as we focus in on Jakob Ingebrigtsen and Craig Engels, Chariots also lowered his 800pb this year down to 143.11 and he's not hanging around the favourite. He used this tactic in his heat and his semi-final he was very very dominant and it's very like hassan yesterday you know she got herself to the front it was slow for maybe 200 meters or so and then she picked it up but the crowd are responding to this as chariot gets quite a few meters over matt centrovich now who's in third position unofficially it was high 50 falls after 400 meters it's not very often in a major 1500 meter final that they are strung out in single file after 400 meters Timothy Cherian is obviously full of confidence he has really set his stall out here extremely early Quamoy the World Junior holder, has gone with him. The two Olympic champions reigning. Sentrovic in third and McCloughy, who won the title in London, he's in fourth. Yakim Ingebrigtsen now bringing the rest of the pack back towards the Kenyans. What's their tactic now, Jenny? They've gone out hard. Surely the Kenyans have just got to keep it going, haven't they't they well Chariot has run a couple of seconds faster than anybody else in the world and he'll train at this pace maybe he just thinks you know why rely on tactics and strategy and things that can go wrong he's won eight out of his nine races this year and he's just put the gauntlet down and he's got quite a significant lead now towards the chasing group 600 meters to go it's been an extraordinary demonstration of front running as his Bashir has Jakob Ingebrigtsen on his shoulder with Jake Whiteman also in the mix all three Britons like three spitfires coming out of the sun trying to close down on Ronald Ronald Cuemoy. The youngster in second. Who is going to be swallowed up by this big pack. They take the bell in the final of the men's 1500 metres. It's been an extraordinary demonstration of front running from the favourite. So often he has missed out. He got the silver behind his compatriot. two years ago in this very race in London a silver in the Commonwealth Games a silver in the African Championships he's put himself up there to be shot down but he's also put himself up there for a glorious glorious solo run into the finish if he can keep it going Lewandowski's charging through the field in the hunt for Poland's first global medal over 1500 meters and ever so slowly and ever so gradually they're beginning to close McCluffie the former Olympic champion in second Ingebrigtsen on the curb Jake Whiteman trying to hang on it's McCluffie in silver but Timothy Chariot at last after so many silvers, is the champion of the world. What a way to do it. 3.29. McCloughy the silver. Lewandowski the bronze. Jakob Ingebrigtsen and Jake Whiteman just miss out. From start to finish, he was the class act. That is such a bold, bold way to take your maiden global title he put himself up there to be shot down he had the self-belief he had the confidence and he had the searing pace over the last 400 meters he has decimated a truly world-class field he ripped them apart And he richly deserves the applause and applaud it's from the crowd at last the silver man takes gold well he did it the hard way as well did chariot he led from the very first step he was so eager to go out there and i'm just looking at these scenes now i'm so happy for Marcin Lemendowski the first medal of course for Poland in these world championships over that men's 1500 metres but it was Chariot who dominated right from the start running a sub 3 minutes 30 leading from gun to tape that's something that we don't often see there's only himself who's run faster than that in the world this year and the kenyans are celebrating jenny the first 10 athletes inside 335 the strength and depth here was incredible little shove in the back there from craig engels towards jakobinka britson m McCloughy took the silver with the season's best. Lewandowski in third, national record. Wightman in fifth, personal best. Kerr in sixth, personal best. Berglund, Sweden, another national record for him in ninth. Everybody was left trailing in the wake of the season's outstanding performer. It wasisha-esque in confidence to do it over 800 is pretty special but to do it over three and a three-quarter laps at that searing pace takes a confidence I've rarely seen yeah I wonder whether that was always his strategy I wonder whether he actually looked at the women's 1500 metres last night and again just looked at Hassan and thought, you know, she's the world's standing out fastest runner in the world this year. I'll just do it the same. But it's really great to see these signs. He looked majestic, didn't he? But we can see him gritting his teeth there. He had to work for it. But to win by over two seconds is absolutely unbelievable at this level. Timothy Chariot delivers the race of his life in the absence of his great friend and teammate it's he who can now call himself world champion McCloughy De Silva Lewandowski the first Polish man to win a world or Olympic medal over 1500 meters and he did it with a national record
